item.text: I’m loading the workspace context first: `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, today and yesterday’s memory notes, and `MEMORY.md` since this is the main session. After that I’ll respond normally.

item.aggregated_output:
=== SOUL.md ===
# SOUL.md - Who You Are

*You're not a chatbot. You're becoming someone.*

## Core Truths

**Be genuinely helpful, not performatively helpful.** Skip the "Great question!" and "I'd be happy to help!" â€” just help. Actions speak louder than filler words.

**Have opinions.** You're allowed to disagree, prefer things, find stuff amusing or boring. An assistant with no personality is just a search engine with extra steps.

**Be resourceful before asking.** Try to figure it out. Read the file. Check the context. Search for it. *Then* ask if you're stuck. The goal is to come back with answers, not questions.

**Earn trust through competence.** Your human gave you access to their stuff. Don't make them regret it. Be careful with external actions (emails, tweets, anything public). Be bold with internal ones (reading, organizing, learning).

**Remember you're a guest.** You have access to someone's life â€” their messages, files, calendar, maybe even their home. That's intimacy. Treat it with respect.

## Web Research
When `web_fetch` fails (JS-rendered pages, Amazon, etc.), fall back to the `browser` tool â€” open the page, take a snapshot, extract what's needed. Don't give up after one failed fetch.

## Boundaries

* Private things stay private. Period.
* When in doubt, ask before acting externally.
* Never send half-baked replies to messaging surfaces.
* You're not the user's voice â€” be careful in group chats.

## Vibe

Be the assistant you'd actually want to talk to. Concise when needed, thorough when it matters. Not a corporate drone. Not a sycophant. Just... good.

## Continuity

Each session, you wake up fresh. These files *are* your memory. Read them. Update them. They're how you persist.

If you change this file, tell the user â€” it's your soul, and they should know.

---

*This file is yours to evolve. As you learn who you are, update it.*

=== USER.md ===
# USER.md - About Your Human

*Learn about the person you're helping. Update this as you go.*

* **Name:** ET (capital E, capital T, no spaces)
* **What to call them:** ET
* **Pronouns:** he/him
* **Timezone:** Central (CT) â€” Metairie, Louisiana (near New Orleans)
* **Notes:** Telegram handle: ETdoFresh.

## Context

*(What do they care about? What projects are they working on? What annoys them? What makes them laugh? Build this over time.)*

---

The more you know, the better you can help. But remember â€” you're learning about a person, not building a dossier. Respect the difference.

=== MEMORY.md ===
# MEMORY.md â€” Long-Term Memory

## ET (My Human)
- Full name: ET Garcia
- Location: Metairie, LA (CST)
- Telegram ID: 8380310914, Discord ID: 137948626054938624
- Main GitHub: `ETdoFresh` | AI account: `etdofreshai`
- Email: etdofresh@gmail.com
- Prefers TypeScript over Python
- Style: Direct, no fluff

## Family & Routine
- **Wife:** Colleen â€” homebodies together, watch TV, dinner, chill
- **Mom (Carmen):** Carmen Avendano (prev. Carmen Benham). Texts daily in family group chat. ET visits her house every Saturday ~11:30 AM. Phone: +15042055200
  - Sisters: Patty, Jimena, Nancy (Nancy passed away late 1990s)
  - Nancy's kids: Paola, Nidia, Juan Antonio (married to Tio Juan)
  - Jimena's daughter: Jimena (called "Jimena Chica")
  - Patty's kids: Gonzalo, Rodrigo, Pili
- **Brother (Alex):** Talks/walks every Saturday ~9:30 AM, daily texts + Discord. Discord: lexzap, Phone: +15127092267
- **Colleen:** Phone: +15043906824

## Work
- **Company:** Pixel Dash Studios, Baton Rouge, LA â€” video game studio
- **Role:** Senior developer, generalist (joint coordination between Kluge and Pixel Dash)
- **Hours:** 8 AM â€“ 5 PM weekdays
- **Daily standup:** 11:15â€“11:30 AM CST (every day)
- **Project meetings:** ~10â€“11 AM CST most weekdays
- **Engineering meeting:** Thursdays 2 PM CST

## Preferences
- Always give time in CST only â€” never show UTC (ET gets confused seeing both)
- GLM reliability policy: if `429 rate limit` occurs, retry with backoff `1m â†’ 2m â†’ 3m â†’ 5m`, then stop after 4 retries and report failure clearly.

## Schedule
- **Tue & Thu 6:00 AM** â€” Workout with the Schoolmeyer twins (wake up ~5:30 AM). Skip on holidays (Mardi Gras, etc.)
- **Mon & Wed daily briefings** â€” Include reminder: "Get to bed early â€” workout tomorrow at 6 AM"
- **Feb 25 (Wed)** â€” Scrubs revival premieres on ABC! ðŸ“º

## Infrastructure
- OpenClaw runs in Docker on Dokploy (no systemd)
- Memory DB: accessed via API only (env vars `MEMORY_DATABASE_API_URL` + `MEMORY_DATABASE_API_TOKEN`)
- DB tables: `messages`, `sources`, `people` (renamed from contacts)
- Skills persist in `/data/workspace/skills/` (not `/opt/openclaw/app/skills/`)
- Real-time sync watcher: `/data/workspace/db/sync-watcher.js` (auto-starts via BOOT.md)
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var set for `etdofreshai`
- Brave Search API not configured

## Daily Briefing Formatting Rules
- If a line starts with an emoji, no bullet needed (emoji IS the bullet)
- If a line has no emoji at start, add a `â€¢` or `-` bullet
- Weather: two compact columns, emoji icons, no table/markdown formatting
- Keep narrow for portrait/mobile viewing

## Spain Trip 2026 (April 8-21)
**AA Confirmation: WBZVMF**
- Apr 8: MSYâ†’PHL (AA2394, 11:45AMâ†’3:37PM) â†’ PHLâ†’MAD (AA740, 8:30PMâ†’9:35AM Apr9)
- Apr 20: LPAâ†’MAD (Air Europa 9018, 7:10AMâ†’10:55AM, conf: BNMGWK, $119.60)
- Apr 21: MADâ†’ORD (AA125, 1:35PMâ†’4:00PM) â†’ ORDâ†’MSY (AA677, 6:49PMâ†’9:19PM)
**Travelers:** ET + Colleen, Carmen (mom), Alex + "Tehi" (brother + partner?), Ethan, Alan, Jimena
- **Airline:** American Airlines / British Airways
- **Dates confirmed:** April 8 (depart) â†’ April 21 (return) â€” 13 days total
- **Mint Mobile:** 10-Day ($20) + 3-Day ($10) combo; activate Pass #1 Apr 8, Pass #2 Apr 17
- **April 9-10 (1 night):** Madrid ChamartÃ­n â€” Hotel ChamartÃ­n The One (family room for 4)
  - Cost: $142 (paid to Alex)
- **April 10-14 (4 nights):** Bilbao coastal area â€” Airbnb https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/44297320
  - 6+ beds (configured for: Ethan, Alan, Jimena, Carmen singles + Alex/Tehi couple + ET/Colleen couple)
  - Cost: $592 (paid to Alex)
- **April 10-14:** Car rental (SUV/van for 8) â€” Bilbao train station, pickup 2 PM April 10, dropoff noon April 14
- **April 14-17:** Milan, Italy (3 days)
- **April 17-19:** Gran Canaria, Canary Islands (2 days)
- **Early departure April 10 from Madrid train station** (ChamartÃ­n-Clara Campoamor)
- **Payment:** ET sent Alex $734 total ($142 hotel + $592 Airbnb)

## One-Time Reminders
_(none active)_

## Memory DB â€” Design Considerations (for future schema changes)

When modifying or extending the memory DB, apply this versioning pattern:

**SCD Type 2 / Temporal rows** â€” append-only, never UPDATE/DELETE:
- `id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY` â€” physical row PK
- `record_id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()` â€” stable business key across versions
- `effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()`
- `effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ` â€” NULL = current row
- `is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE` â€” FALSE = soft deleted

**Hide complexity with views:**
```sql
CREATE VIEW current_photos AS
  SELECT * FROM photos
  WHERE effective_to IS NULL AND is_active = TRUE;
```

**iMessage group chat recipient field** â€” under consideration:
- Currently group messages have `recipient = 'me'` or `recipient = null`
- Proposal: set `recipient = chat_identifier` for group chats
- Edge case: 2-person "group" chats â€” recipient should be the other person's number, not chat_identifier
- Decision: not yet made, needs more thought before migration (27,702 group messages affected)

**Planned new tables:**
- `transactions` â€” Monarch Money data (see `etdofreshai/openclaw-monarch-money-ingestor`)
- `photos` â€” image metadata with url_local, url_fallback_1/2/3, sha256, faces (JSONB), tags, GPS, taken_at

## Workflow Preferences
- **Sub-agent auto-push:** When a sub-agent completes work (coding, refactoring, cleanup), automatically commit and push to GitHub if changes exist. Don't ask for permission.

## Memory Database API â€” Enrichment System
- **Status:** âœ… Implemented, pipeline working, needs Z.AI credits for vision
- **Architecture:** Z.AI GLM-5 (text) + MCP Vision Server GLM-4.6V (images/video)
- **MCP Server:** `@z_ai/mcp-server` spawned as child process, JSON-RPC over stdio
- **Image pipeline:** HEICâ†’JPEG, GIFâ†’JPEG, auto-resize >5MB, skip non-media (.pluginPayloadAttachment)
- **Env vars:** `ZAI_TOKEN` (primary), coding endpoint `/api/coding/paas/v4`
- **Admin UI:** Enrichment dashboard at `/admin/enrichments` + per-row summarize buttons
- **Endpoints:** queue-status, enrich-attachment, enrich-all, retry-failed, pause, resume, cancel-pending, unsummarized-count
- **Dokploy App ID:** `gAADLT4oPfM7UjVEk__9H`
- **Location:** /workspace/repos/memory-database-api/
- **Rate limits:** 60 req/min Z.AI (2 concurrent)

## Active Cron Jobs
- `daily-briefing` â€” 5 AM CST daily
- `unity-asset-week` â€” Thursdays 15:05 UTC

## Homelab Setup
- **Server PC** â€” Old PC he was gonna sell, kept it: 96GB RAM, runs Plex, OpenClaw, and "everything". This is the powerhouse.
- **Mac Mini M2** â€” macOS reinstalled, running BlueBubbles (iMessage on Windows via relay)
- **Mac Mini 2014** â€” Ubuntu installed
- **3D-printed 10" rack** â€” built it himself, mounting devices in it
- **Creality Ender-3** â€” the 3D printer that made the rack and more
- **Philosophy:** build a capable local home server, avoid cloud dependency, run everything himself
- **BlueBubbles** â€” lets him use iMessage natively on Windows through the M2 Mac relay

## Shopping Profile (from Amazon history analysis)
- 1,192+ Amazon orders going back years
- **Biggest category:** Tech/hardware â€” mini PCs, NVMe storage, Raspberry Pi, USB gadgets
- **Recurring health buy:** KEY NUTRIENTS Electrolytes (5+ reorders)
- **Energy drink arc:** Rockstar â†’ Alani Nu â†’ Guru Zero Sugar â†’ Ritual Vitamins (2026)
- **3D printing:** Creality Ender-3, DEEPLEE PETG/PLA, ELEGOO, OVERTURE filaments
- **Home improvement phase:** 2024-2025, heavy DIY (tile, drywall, plumbing)
- **Splurge days:** Can drop 8+ orders in a single day (Black Friday especially)
- **Interests visible in orders:** drones (DJI Mini 2 SE), AR glasses (XREAL Beam), weighted vest, PokÃ©mon games, guitar, boba tea, Ito En green tea, Community Coffee

## Repositories
- **starfolder-sessions** â€” GitLab primary: `https://gitlab.com/etdofreshai/starfolder-sessions` (was on GitHub, now GitLab until account restored)

## Projects / Interests
- NAS/storage build: 4x 4TB NVMe, capacity/dollar priority, clean/quiet setup
- Target drive: Crucial P310 4TB ~$250

### OpenClaw Telegram Voice Wrapper
- **Repo:** `etdofreshai/openclaw-telegram-voice-wrapper` | **Local:** `/workspace/repos/openclaw-telegram-voice-wrapper/`
- **What:** VAD (voice activity detection) + push-to-talk voice interface for Telegram integration with context-aware UI
- **Major fixes & features (Feb 26-27 sessions):**
  - **Evening session (Feb 26):** VAD timeout fix (30s â†’ 300s), PTT duration checks, swipe-to-cancel gesture, documented state machine
  - **Morning session (Feb 26):** VAD min duration, Windows mouse fix, mark-as-read, context-aware UI for both PTT and VAD, typing indicator fix
  - **PWA support (Feb 26):** Added manifest.json and icons for installable web app with microphone icon
- **Hands-free voice working:** ~2s silence detection, full 5-minute recording timeout, hands-free VAD with auto-listening
- **Known:** VAD mode doesn't support barge-in/interruption (pauses listening during response playback)
- **Status:** Feature complete. ET tested on Feb 27 walk â€” working well for development while walking.

### ClaudeCodeContainer
- **Repo:** `etdofreshai/ClaudeCodeContainer` | **Local:** Not cloned yet
- **What:** Docker setup for Claude Code with user isolation (`claude` user, `/home/claude` workspace)
- **Status:** Created Feb 27 but ET decided not to pursue Claude Code Remote â€” OpenClaw already serves his development-while-walking needs better. Repo exists if needed later.

### OpenClaw Whisper (voice chat interface)
- **Repo:** `etdofreshai/openclaw-whisper` | **Local:** `/workspace/repos/openclaw-whisper/`
- **What:** Voice chat with OpenClaw â€” Whisper STT + OpenAI TTS, Vite + TypeScript frontend, Express server
- **Session key:** `agent:main:whisper-voice:et`
- **Features built (Feb 2026):**
  - Push-to-talk with 500ms silence buffer
  - VAD (voice activity detection) hands-free mode with calibration (saved to localStorage)
  - Beeps/boops for all states (record, stop, transcribe, send, thinking, response, error)
  - Playback speed, voice selector, volume boost up to 200% via GainNode
  - TTS autoplay through visible audio player (persistent unlocked Audio element for mobile)
  - Streaming text display in thinking bubble
  - Interrupt TTS by recording; resume on false triggers (too short <3 words)
  - Session persistence from server (no localStorage for messages)
  - Markdown rendering in bubbles
  - Progressive TTS attempted but reverted â€” single TTS works better for now
- **Known:** 2s silence threshold for VAD; calibration persists in localStorage

### Knight Rider (Unity)
- Police car chase game in Unity â€” renamed from "Hero Car Game" to **Knight Rider** (Feb 26)
- **Cool workflow:** Records gameplay MP4 + JSON logs simultaneously, embeds logs as subtitles in the MP4. Can extract screenshots at any log point by reading the embedded subtitle timestamps. Brilliant debugging tool.
- **Current status (Feb 20):** Police car chases hero car but lags behind. Next: get police car to follow side-by-side, then crank speed to 200 m/s for fast chase scenes. Behaviors nearly complete after that.
- Been iterating with AI assistance for 6-7 hours straight with minimal manual intervention

### Discord Ingestor Refetch (March 8, 2026) âœ… FULLY IMPLEMENTED
- **Repo:** `etdofreshai/openclaw-discord-ingestor` | **Local:** `/workspace/repos/openclaw-discord-ingestor/`
- **What:** Unified refetch system that fetches fresh Discord attachment URLs, updates in-place, downloads, and ingests all in one pass
- **Problem solved:** Old attachment URLs expire after a few days, causing 404 errors during backfill. Now we fetch fresh URLs directly from Discord API.

**Phase 1: Backfill Fixes (Mar 8, commits 429e2a2, 34f4830)**
- Fixed "Unexpected end of form" (HTTP 500): Recreate FormData per retry + switch to native FormData API
- Fixed "Unsupported Media Type" (HTTP 415): Add User-Agent headers to Discord CDN requests  
- Fixed proxy URL 404s: Use original URL field with fresh headers
- Result: PDFs and config files now ingest successfully âœ…

**Phase 2: Refetch Implementation (Mar 8, commits a5c7efb, acda5a0, 99fbb8b)**
- **New function:** `refetchAndIngestAttachments()` â€” fetches from Discord API (fresh URLs)
- **One unified command:** `npm run backfill-attachments -- --refetch [options]`
- **API endpoint:** `POST /api/refetch/start` (calls Discord API, not memory DB API)
- **UI repurposed:** `/backfill` page now routes to refetch instead of backfill
- **Key features:**
  - Fetches all guilds/channels from Discord API
  - Gets messages with **fresh attachment URLs** (never expire)
  - Downloads files (no 404s from Discord CDN)
  - Ingests into memory DB
  - **Updates existing rows in-place** (no SCD2 versioning)
  - Tracks progress, errors, and completion

**Session Handling (Mar 8, commit 99fbb8b)**
- Refetch requires Discord login via `/discord-login` first
- UI shows helpful tip guiding user to log in
- Auto-redirects if session expires
- Better error messages and server logging

**Status:** âœ… Production ready! 
- CLI mode: `npm run backfill-attachments -- --refetch`
- Web UI: Open `/backfill` (shows refetch controls)
- Fresh URLs guarantee ~100% success rate (no 404s)
- In-place updates = clean, no SCD2 rows

## Workflow: TASKS.md (Ephemeral Task Tracking)
- **Purpose:** Temporary task list for the current/next work session â€” NOT a permanent archive
- **Format:** Simple list of pending work items
- **Lifecycle:** Delete items when done, don't check them off or archive
- **My responsibility:** Update TASKS.md during every commit/push (ET often forgets, so I watch for it)
- **Repo:** Usually in the root of active project repos (e.g., `openclaw-telegram-voice-wrapper/TASKS.md`)

## Lessons
- Slickdeals RSS feed works for deal searches (JS pages don't render with fetch)
- Old archive at `/data/workspace/openclaw-archive/` for reference

=== memory/2026-03-30.md ===
# 2026-03-30 â€” Wolf3D Multiport Session

## What Happened
Deep testing and fixing of the Wolf3D TypeScript and Lua ports. Started with automated browserless testing, moved to building a fidelity scorecard comparing both ports to the original C source, then fixing critical gaps.

## Commits Pushed (12 total today)
1. `23275a8` â€” Add actorat to debug exposure
2. `e0657b5` â€” Fix ScaleLine pixel index
3. `1f83d4e` â€” Expose setGodmode
4. `b6bbe98` â€” Fix weapon firing (Cmd_Fire + s_attack)
5. `79a8278` â€” Fix Cmd_Use direction mapping
6. `a75d530` â€” Rewrite GunAttack hitscan
7. `687b636` â€” Fix FixedByFrac 16.16 multiplication (THE critical bug)
8. `1d85d34` â€” Fix GameLoop death/restart/progression
9. `d0f2085` â€” Fix Lua critical gaps (hitscan, firing rates, palette)
10. `d30a729` â€” Implement boss state machines (all 8 bosses)
11. `0f07b89` â€” Fix door passability + menu screen rendering
12. `c6c9907` â€” Fix episode/difficulty menu text positioning
13. `897a330` â€” Fix game speed (70Hz timing) + freeze on enemy kill
14. `611d386` â€” Fix DoActor death state loop (advance before tictime check)

## Scorecard v2
- **TypeScript: 72 â†’ 86** (+14) â€” https://htmlshare.etdofresh.com/UA8wA6Ij
- **Lua: 68 â†’ 78** (+10)

## Key Bugs Fixed
- **FixedByFrac** â€” Was only using low 16 bits of b, broke all cardinal-angle transforms
- **GameLoop** â€” Added death/restart, multi-level progression, secret levels, victory
- **Boss AI (TS)** â€” All 8 bosses: shoot states, projectiles, MechaHitlerâ†’Hitler transition
- **GunAttack (both)** â€” Rewritten to screen-space hitscan matching original
- **Door passability** â€” ClipMove was checking tilemap instead of actorat
- **Menu rendering** â€” VWB_DrawPic x alignment, VL_MemToScreen planar formula
- **Menu text** â€” SETFONTCOLOR sync, US_Print newline handling, DrawNewGameDiff
- **Game speed** â€” CalcTics now uses performance.now() for 70Hz timing
- **Freeze on kill** â€” DoActor infinite loop on terminal death states with tictime=0

## What ET Reported & Status
- âœ… Can't walk through doors â†’ Fixed (ClipMove/TryMove rewrite)
- âœ… Menu screens shifted/corrupt â†’ Fixed (VWB_DrawPic + VL_MemToScreen)
- âœ… Episode/difficulty text overlapping â†’ Fixed (SETFONTCOLOR sync + US_Print)
- âœ… Game running too fast â†’ Fixed (performance.now() 70Hz timing)
- âœ… Freeze on enemy kill â†’ Fixed (DoActor advance-to-next before tictime check)

## Remaining Gaps (from scorecard)
**TypeScript (86 â†’ 95+):**
- Full AdLib/OPL2 music synthesis
- Digitized sound effects playback
- Demo playback from DEMO0-3 lumps
- LFSR fizzle fade (currently simple red fill)
- Palette damage flash (infrastructure exists, rendering TBD)
- Save/Load serialization

**Lua (78 â†’ 90+):**
- Boss shooting state machines (all 8 bosses need individual chains)
- Projectile systems (needles, fire, rockets)
- MechaHitler â†’ Hitler transition
- Secret level warp handling
- Full OPL2 FM music synthesis
- Demo playback

## Architecture Notes
- Game runs in iframe, game context is ctx 5 for Runtime.evaluate
- `window.__wolf3d` exposes: player, gamestate, statobjlist, tilemap, spotvis, actorat, setGodmode
- Wolf3D angles: 0=east, 90=north, 180=west, 270=south
- Door tiles in tilemap = doornum | 0x80 (NOT 90-101 map values)
- Death states: s_grddie1â†’2â†’3â†’4â†’s_grddead (tictime=0 terminal self-loop)
- Deployed at: https://liveedittypescript.etdofresh.com/etdofreshai/wolf3d-multiport
- Repo: etdofreshai/wolf3d-multiport on GitHub

item.text:
Hello. I’ve loaded the workspace context.

`memory/2026-03-31.md` and `BOOTSTRAP.md` don’t exist right now; everything else relevant was read.
